channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ecuador's conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer advance to presidential runoff
item.description: GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuador will choose its next president in a runoff election after conservative incumbent Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González garnered enough votes Sunday to beat 14 other candidates.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: President Trump's message for Chiefs, Taylor Swift after Super Bowl LIX and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Black educators warn of what is lost if Education Department is shuttered
item.description: Black educators are sounding the alarm as President Donald Trump ramps up talk of eliminating the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A 7.6 magnitude earthquake shakes the Caribbean, some islands urge residents to leave coastlines
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A magnitude-7.6 earthquake shook the Caribbean Sea south of the Cayman Islands Saturday, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. Several islands and countries urged people near the coastline to move inland but authorities in most places later lifted the tsunami alerts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cruise ship with more than 90 sick following outbreak docks in Tampa
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is investigating why more than 90 cruise passengers got sick on a Royal Caribbean ship that docked in Tampa on Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Young, NewsNation

item.title: With Trump in the White House, Iranians mark the anniversary of the 1979 Islamic Revolution
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Tens of thousands of Iranians marked the anniversary of the country's 1979 Islamic Revolution, the first such rally since President Donald Trump returned to the White House and restarted his “maximum pressure” campaign targeting Tehran.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he has directed US Treasury to stop minting new pennies, citing rising cost
item.description: President Donald Trump says he has directed the Treasury Department to stop minting new pennies, citing the rising cost of producing the one-cent coin.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are focusing on the wreckage of a plane that crashed off western Alaska as they try to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea, killing 10 people.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court grants request to block detained Venezuelan immigrants from being sent to Guantanamo
item.description: A federal court has blocked the Trump administration from sending three Venezuelan immigrants held in New Mexico to Guantanamo Bay Naval Base in Cuba as part of the president’s immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: PHOTOS: Celebrities spotted at the Super Bowl
item.description: The players on the field Sunday at Super Bowl LIX are celebrities in their own right, but they're far from the only big names gathered in New Orleans for the Chiefs-Eagles matchup.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marine from California among those killed in Philippines surveillance mission
item.description: Marine Corps officials at Camp Pendleton on Sunday identified Sgt. Jacob M. Durham as the U.S. Marine who tragically lost his life in an aircraft incident on Feb. 6, 2025, in the Philippine Province of Maguindanao del Sur.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, KTLA 5

item.title: Eagles beat Chiefs 40-22 in lopsided Super Bowl LIX
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs wanted to make history with three back-to-back-to-back championships, but Super Bowl LIX was dominated by the Philadelphia Eagles, who won the game 40-22.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says 25 percent tariffs on steel and aluminum coming Monday
item.description: Trump said that the steel and aluminum tariffs would impact “everybody” when asked what countries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KREX 5

item.title: Trump says he is serious about Canada becoming 51st state in Super Bowl interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is serious about wanting Canada to become the 51st state in an interview that aired Sunday during the Super Bowl preshow.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump starts term with positive approval rating in CBS poll
item.description: In the CBS/YouGov poll, 53 percent said that when it comes to “the way Donald Trump is handling his job as president,” they support it.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump official orders consumer protection agency to stop work
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down an agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said. The Nome Volunteer Fire Department made the announcement on its Facebook page Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Efforts to put carbon dioxide underground face less support in Trump's second term
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Putting carbon dioxide from power plants and industrial facilities underground where it won’t contribute to global warming could see less federal support and enthusiasm under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Snow blankets parts of Northeast US, up to a foot expected for some
item.description: A strong storm is expected to bring moderate to heavy snowfall across parts of the Upper Great Lakes on Saturday before intensifying overnight across the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Elon Musk is calling for the impeachment of the federal judge who made a decision early Saturday morning that the Treasury Department should block access to anyone “other than civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties” from its payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KRON4

item.title: DC plane crash victim and Kansas City Chiefs superfan planned return home for family Super Bowl party
item.description: Family and friends confirm a victim of the deadly American Airlines and Army helicopter plane crash was an avid Kansas City Chiefs fan with plans to watch the Super Bowl with his family.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unusual video from Florida police shows note on 'first date' brick woman allegedly hurled through ex's window
item.description: Local authorities in Florida release an unusual video of a woman throwing a brick through her ex-boyfriend's window after she initially denied the act and was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As female representation hits new highs among states, constitutions still assume officials are male
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — South Dakota’s constitution refers to the governor as a “he.” But for the past six years, the governor was a “she” — Kristi Noem, the first female to hold the position there. The conservative Republican pushed a constitutional amendment to change the gendered language.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House Republicans mull taxes on scholarships and changes to student loan programs
item.description: As Republicans in Congress look for ways to slash spending, some legislators are floating new taxes on college scholarships, an end to student loan repayment plans and a big hike in taxes on university endowments.
item.dc:creator: CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: Hawaii’s sunshine, short rain showers and clean air together create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mcavoy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Curry scores 34 points, Butler adds 25 in Golden State debut as Warriors rally to beat Bulls 132-111
item.description: Stephen Curry scored 34 points and tied a season high with eight 3-pointers, Jimmy Butler added 25 points in his Golden State debut and the Warriors rallied to beat the Chicago Bulls 132-111 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Philadelphia radio station bans Taylor Swift music ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: A release from the station claims that 94.5 is "pressing pause on all things Taylor Swift." The announcement came after a Swift fan and a Philly advertising agency spent the week trolling each other with ads in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, ABC 4

item.title: Super Bowl LIX: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce spotted out in New Orleans
item.description: Power couple Taylor Swift and Kansas City Chiefs Tight End Travis Kelce were spotted on Friday night eating at a restaurant while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LeBron James will sit out the Lakers' game against Indiana to rest his sore left ankle
item.description: LeBron James is sitting out the Los Angeles Lakers ' game against Indiana on Saturday to rest his sore left ankle.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UNLV shooter kept dresses for sex workers, received startling text in aftermath, Las Vegas police say
item.description: The man who shot and killed three UNLV professors kept dozens of dresses for sex workers in his apartment and received a startling text message in the aftermath of the mass shooting, according to the Las Vegas Metro police final report released Friday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: Latino group calls for boycott of Coca-Cola, Walmart and more over deportations
item.description: This is part of an effort by a group called Freeze Latino Movement to boycott American companies while poking fun of the ongoing deportations launched by the Trump Administration and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KRON4

item.title: Senate confirms Project 2025 architect Russell Vought to lead powerful White House budget office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Russell Vought as White House budget director on Thursday night, putting an official who has planned the zealous expansion of President Donald Trump's power into one of the most influential positions in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4
